Scenario.overview: Communications Industry Simple This scenario outlines a simplified cyber attack similar to the 2015 Ukraine Electric Power Attack, where attackers gained initial access through supply chain compromise, executed malware to take control of systems, and caused impact by destructively altering data.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Malware Repository, File
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Malware Metadata (Mechanism), File Modification (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attackers compromised a trusted third-party supplier to gain initial access to the communication company's network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Once inside, the attackers delivered malicious software via email attachments to key systems, allowing them to execute commands and control affected machines.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attackers executed their payload to destroy critical data, disrupting the communication services and rendering systems unusable.

Scenario.summary: In this simplified cyber attack, an expert-level state-sponsored group exploited a trusted supplier to infiltrate a communication company's network. They then deployed malware to seize control of systems and ultimately destroyed critical data, causing significant disruption to the company's operations.
